name.desc: Workflow name that appears in the GitHub Actions interface.

description.desc: Optional workflow description that is rendered as a comment in the generated GitHub Actions YAML file (.lock.yml)

emoji.desc: Optional emoji to represent the workflow visually in listings and UI surfaces.

source.desc: Optional source reference indicating where this workflow was added from.

redirect.desc: Optional workflow location redirect for updates.

tracker-id.desc: Optional tracker identifier to tag all created assets (issues, discussions, comments, pull requests).

labels.desc: Optional array of labels to categorize and organize workflows.

metadata.desc: Optional metadata field for storing custom key-value pairs compatible with the custom agent spec.

aw.desc: Array of shared agentic workflow specifications to import.

resources.desc: Optional list of additional workflow or action files that should be fetched alongside this workflow when running 'gh ...

inlined-imports.desc: If true, inline all imports (including those without inputs) at compilation time in the generated lock.yml instead of...

on.desc: Workflow triggers that define when the agentic workflow should run.

slash_command.desc: Special slash command trigger for /command workflows (e.g., '/my-bot' in issue comments).

label_command.desc: On Label Command trigger: fires when a specific label is added to an issue, pull request, or discussion.

push.desc: Push event trigger that runs the workflow when code is pushed to the repository

pull_request.desc: Pull request event trigger that runs the workflow when pull requests are created, updated, or closed

issues.desc: Issues event trigger that runs when repository issues are created, updated, or managed

discussion.desc: Discussion event trigger that runs the workflow when repository discussions are created, updated, or managed

discussion_comment.desc: Discussion comment event trigger that runs the workflow when comments on discussions are created, updated, or deleted

schedule.desc: Scheduled trigger events using fuzzy schedules or standard cron expressions.

pull_request_review_comment.desc: Pull request review comment event trigger

branch_protection_rule.desc: Branch protection rule event trigger that runs when branch protection rules are changed

check_run.desc: Check run event trigger that runs when a check run is created, rerequested, completed, or has a requested action

check_suite.desc: Check suite event trigger that runs when check suite activity occurs

create.desc: Create event trigger that runs when a Git reference (branch or tag) is created

delete.desc: Delete event trigger that runs when a Git reference (branch or tag) is deleted

deployment.desc: Deployment event trigger that runs when a deployment is created

deployment_status.desc: Deployment status event trigger that runs when a deployment status is updated

fork.desc: Fork event trigger that runs when someone forks the repository

gollum.desc: Gollum event trigger that runs when someone creates or updates a Wiki page

label.desc: Label event trigger that runs when a label is created, edited, or deleted

merge_group.desc: Merge group event trigger that runs when a pull request is added to a merge queue

milestone.desc: Milestone event trigger that runs when a milestone is created, closed, opened, edited, or deleted

page_build.desc: Page build event trigger that runs when someone pushes to a GitHub Pages publishing source branch

public.desc: Public event trigger that runs when a repository changes from private to public

pull_request_target.desc: Pull request target event trigger that runs in the context of the base repository (secure for fork PRs)

pull_request_review.desc: Pull request review event trigger that runs when a pull request review is submitted, edited, or dismissed

registry_package.desc: Registry package event trigger that runs when a package is published or updated

repository_dispatch.desc: Repository dispatch event trigger for custom webhook events

status.desc: Status event trigger that runs when the status of a Git commit changes

watch.desc: Watch event trigger that runs when someone stars the repository

workflow_call.desc: Workflow call event trigger that allows this workflow to be called by another workflow

stop-after.desc: Time when workflow should stop running.

skip-if-match.desc: Conditionally skip workflow execution when a GitHub search query has matches.

skip-if-no-match.desc: Conditionally skip workflow execution when a GitHub search query has no matches (or fewer than minimum).

skip-if-check-failing.desc: Skip workflow execution if any CI checks on the target branch are failing or pending.

skip-roles.desc: Skip workflow execution for users with specific repository roles.

skip-bots.desc: Skip workflow execution for specific GitHub users.

skip-author-associations.desc: Skip workflow execution when an event-specific payload author_association field (for example: github.event.comment.au...

roles.desc: Repository access roles required to trigger agentic workflows.

bots.desc: Allow list of bot identifiers that can trigger the workflow even if they don't meet the required role permissions.

labels.desc: Filter workflows triggered by pull_request_target (or other labeled events) to only fire when the triggering label ma...

allow-bot-authored-trigger-comment.desc: Allow the bot-posted-menu / user-checks-box pattern: when a workflow posts a checkbox-menu comment as a GitHub App bo...

manual-approval.desc: Environment name that requires manual approval before the workflow can run.

reaction.desc: AI reaction to add/remove on triggering item.

status-comment.desc: Whether to post status comments (started/completed) on the triggering item.

github-token.desc: Custom GitHub token for pre-activation reactions, activation status comments, and skip-if search queries.

github-app.desc: GitHub App configuration for minting a token used in pre-activation reactions, activation status comments, and skip-i...

needs.desc: Explicit additional custom workflow jobs that pre_activation and activation should depend on.

steps.desc: Steps to inject into the pre-activation job.

permissions.desc: Additional permissions for the pre-activation job.

stale-check.desc: When set to false, disables the frontmatter hash check step in the activation job.

permissions.desc: GitHub token permissions for the workflow.

actions.desc: Permission for GitHub Actions workflows and runs (read: view workflows, write: manage workflows, none: no access)

attestations.desc: Permission for artifact attestations (read: view attestations, write: create attestations, none: no access)

checks.desc: Permission for repository checks and status checks (read: view checks, write: create/update checks, none: no access)

contents.desc: Permission for repository contents (read: view files, write: modify files/branches, none: no access)

deployments.desc: Permission for repository deployments (read: view deployments, write: create/update deployments, none: no access)

discussions.desc: Permission for repository discussions (read: view discussions, write: create/update discussions, none: no access)

id-token.desc: Permission level for OIDC token requests (write/none only - read is not supported).

issues.desc: Permission for repository issues (read: view issues, write: create/update/close issues, none: no access)

models.desc: Permission for GitHub Copilot models (read: access AI models for agentic workflows, none: no access)

metadata.desc: Permission for repository metadata (read: view repository information, write: update repository metadata, none: no ac...

vulnerability-alerts.desc: Permission level for Dependabot vulnerability alerts (read/write/none).

all.desc: Permission shorthand that applies read access to all permission scopes.

run-name.desc: Custom name for workflow runs that appears in the GitHub Actions interface (supports GitHub expressions like ${{ gith...

jobs.desc: Groups together all the jobs that run in the workflow

runs-on.desc: Runner type for workflow execution (GitHub Actions standard field).

group.desc: Runner group name for self-hosted runners or GitHub-hosted runner groups

labels.desc: List of runner labels for self-hosted runners or GitHub-hosted runner selection

runs-on-slim.desc: Runner for all framework/generated jobs (activation, pre-activation, safe-outputs, unlock, APM, etc.).

timeout-minutes.desc: Workflow timeout in minutes (GitHub Actions standard field).

concurrency.desc: Concurrency control to limit concurrent workflow runs (GitHub Actions standard field).

cancel-in-progress.desc: Whether to cancel in-progress workflows in the same concurrency group when a new one starts.

queue.desc: Pending run queue behavior for this concurrency group.

job-discriminator.desc: Additional discriminator expression appended to compiler-generated job-level concurrency groups (agent, output jobs).

inline-sub-agents.desc: Deprecated switch for inline sub-agent support.

features.desc: Feature flags and configuration options for experimental or optional features in the workflow.

models.desc: Named model alias definitions with ordered fallback lists, resolved recursively by AWF.

disable-model-invocation.desc: Controls whether the custom agent should disable model invocation.

secrets.desc: Secret values passed to workflow execution.

environment.desc: Environment that the job references (for protected environments and deployments)

name.desc: The name of the environment configured in the repo

container.desc: Container to run the job steps in

image.desc: The Docker image to use as the container

username.desc: Username for Docker registry authentication when pulling private container images.

password.desc: Password or access token for Docker registry authentication.

ports.desc: Ports to expose on the container

network.desc: Network access control for AI engines using ecosystem identifiers and domain allowlists.

allowed.desc: List of allowed domains or ecosystem identifiers (e.g., 'defaults', 'python', 'node', '*.example.com').

blocked.desc: List of blocked domains or ecosystem identifiers (e.g., 'python', 'node', 'tracker.example.com').

type.desc: Legacy sandbox type field (use agent instead).

agent.desc: Agent sandbox type: 'awf' uses AWF (Agent Workflow Firewall), or false to disable agent sandbox.

id.desc: Agent identifier (replaces 'type' field in new format): 'awf' for Agent Workflow Firewall

type.desc: Legacy: Sandbox type to use (use 'id' instead)

version.desc: AWF version override used to install and run the matching firewall version.

command.desc: Custom command to replace the default AWF installation.

args.desc: Additional arguments to append to the command (applies to AWF, for standard and custom commands)

env.desc: Environment variables to set on the execution step (applies to AWF)

mounts.desc: Container mounts to add when using AWF.

memory.desc: Memory limit for the AWF container (e.g., '4g', '8g').

filesystem.desc: Filesystem access control configuration for the agent within the sandbox.

ignoreViolations.desc: Map of command patterns to paths that should ignore violations

enableWeakerNestedSandbox.desc: Enable weaker nested sandbox mode (recommended: true for Docker access)

config.desc: Legacy custom Sandbox Runtime configuration (use agent.config instead).

filesystem.desc: Filesystem access control configuration for sandboxed workflows.

denyRead.desc: Array of path patterns that deny read access in the sandboxed environment.

allowWrite.desc: Array of path patterns that allow write access in the sandboxed environment.

denyWrite.desc: Array of path patterns that deny write access in the sandboxed environment.

ignoreViolations.desc: When true, log sandbox violations without blocking execution.

enableWeakerNestedSandbox.desc: When true, allows nested sandbox processes to run with relaxed restrictions.

mcp.desc: MCP Gateway configuration for routing MCP server calls through a unified HTTP gateway.

container.desc: Container image for the MCP gateway executable (required)

version.desc: Optional version/tag for the container image (e.g., 'latest', 'v1.0.0')

entrypoint.desc: Optional custom entrypoint for the MCP gateway container.

entrypointArgs.desc: Arguments to add after the container image (container entrypoint arguments)

mounts.desc: Volume mounts for the MCP gateway container.

port.desc: Port number for the MCP gateway HTTP server (default: 8080)

api-key.desc: API key for authenticating with the MCP gateway (supports ${{ secrets.* }} syntax)

domain.desc: Gateway domain for URL generation (default: 'host.docker.internal' when agent is enabled, 'localhost' when disabled)

keepalive-interval.desc: Keepalive ping interval in seconds for HTTP MCP backends.

pre-steps.desc: Custom workflow steps to run at the very beginning of the agent job, before checkout and any other built-in steps.

pre-agent-steps.desc: Custom workflow steps to run immediately before AI execution, after all initialization and setup steps in the agent job.

post-steps.desc: Custom workflow steps to run after AI execution

engine.desc: AI engine configuration that specifies which AI processor interprets and executes the markdown content of the workflow.

id.desc: AI engine identifier: built-in ('claude', 'codex', 'copilot', 'gemini', 'opencode', 'crush', 'pi') or a named catalog...

version.desc: Optional version of the AI engine action (e.g., 'beta', 'stable', 20).

model.desc: Optional specific LLM model to use (e.g., 'claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022', 'gpt-4').

max-turns.desc: Maximum number of chat iterations per run.

max-continuations.desc: Maximum number of continuations for multi-run autopilot mode.

cancel-in-progress.desc: Whether to cancel in-progress runs of the same concurrency group.

queue.desc: Pending run queue behavior for this concurrency group.

user-agent.desc: Custom user agent string for GitHub MCP server configuration (codex engine only)

command.desc: Custom executable path for the AI engine CLI.

harness.desc: Custom Node.js harness script filename for an agentic engine.

env.desc: Custom environment variables to pass to the AI engine, including secret overrides (e.g., OPENAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets....

auth.desc: Engine-level authentication configuration for AWF API proxy sidecar integration (for example, Azure OpenAI via GitHub...

audience.desc: OIDC audience to request from GitHub Actions for token exchange.

azure-tenant-id.desc: Optional Azure tenant ID for token exchange.

azure-client-id.desc: Optional Azure client ID for token exchange.

azure-scope.desc: Optional Azure OAuth scope (defaults to https://cognitiveservices.azure.com/.default in AWF sidecar).

azure-cloud.desc: Optional Azure cloud name (for example, public, usgovernment, china).

config.desc: Additional TOML configuration text that will be appended to the generated config.toml in the action (codex engine only)

agent.desc: Agent identifier to pass to copilot --agent flag (copilot engine only).

api-target.desc: Custom API endpoint hostname for the agentic engine.

token-weights.desc: Custom model token weights for effective token computation.

multipliers.desc: Per-model cost multipliers relative to the reference model (claude-sonnet-4.5 = 1.0).

token-class-weights.desc: Per-token-class weights applied before the model multiplier.

args.desc: Optional array of command-line arguments to pass to the AI engine CLI.

bare.desc: When true, disables automatic loading of context and custom instructions by the AI engine.

session-timeout.desc: Session timeout for MCP gateway sessions as a Go duration string (e.g.

tool-timeout.desc: Timeout for individual MCP tool calls as a Go duration string (e.g.

max-effective-tokens.desc: Explicit ET budget control for firewall cost enforcement.

max-runs.desc: AWF invocation cap (`apiProxy.maxRuns`) applied consistently across all engines.

tools.desc: Tools and MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers available to the AI engine for GitHub API access, browser automation, ...

github.desc: GitHub API tools for repository operations (issues, pull requests, content management)

allowed.desc: List of allowed GitHub API functions (e.g., 'create_issue', 'update_issue', 'add_comment')

type.desc: GitHub MCP transport type: 'local' (Docker-based, default) or 'remote' (hosted at api.githubcopilot.com)

version.desc: Optional version specification for the GitHub MCP server (used with 'local' type).

args.desc: Optional additional arguments to append to the generated MCP server command (used with 'local' type)

read-only.desc: Enable read-only mode to restrict GitHub MCP server to read-only operations only

lockdown.desc: Enable lockdown mode to limit content surfaced from public repositories (only items authored by users with push access).

integrity-proxy.desc: Controls DIFC proxy injection for pre-agent gh CLI steps when guard policies (min-integrity) are configured.

toolsets.desc: GitHub MCP server toolset name(s) to enable.

mounts.desc: Volume mounts for the containerized GitHub MCP server (format: 'host:container:mode' where mode is 'ro' for read-only...

min-integrity.desc: Guard policy: minimum required integrity level for repository access.

blocked-users.desc: Guard policy: GitHub usernames whose content is unconditionally blocked.

trusted-users.desc: Guard policy: GitHub usernames whose content is elevated to 'approved' integrity regardless of author_association.

approval-labels.desc: Guard policy: GitHub label names that promote a content item's effective integrity to 'approved' when present.

endorsement-reactions.desc: Guard policy: GitHub reaction types that promote a content item's integrity to 'approved' when added by maintainers.

disapproval-reactions.desc: Guard policy: GitHub reaction types that demote content integrity when added by maintainers.

endorser-min-integrity.desc: Guard policy: minimum integrity level required for an endorser (reactor) to promote content.

github-app.desc: GitHub App configuration for token minting.

owner.desc: Optional owner of the GitHub App installation (defaults to current repository owner if not specified)

repositories.desc: Optional list of repositories to grant access to (defaults to current repository if not specified)

permissions.desc: Optional extra GitHub App-only permissions to merge into the minted token.

web-fetch.desc: Web content fetching tool for downloading web pages and API responses (subject to network permissions)

web-search.desc: Web search tool for performing internet searches and retrieving search results (subject to network permissions)

grep.desc: DEPRECATED: grep is always available as part of default bash tools.

edit.desc: File editing tool for reading, creating, and modifying files in the repository

playwright.desc: Playwright browser automation tool for web scraping, testing, and UI interactions in containerized browsers

args.desc: Optional additional arguments to append to the generated MCP server command (MCP mode only)

mode.desc: Integration mode: 'cli' (recommended) installs @playwright/cli via npm for token-efficient CLI invocations — use play...

agentic-workflows.desc: GitHub Agentic Workflows MCP server for workflow introspection and analysis.

cache-memory.desc: Cache memory MCP configuration for persistent memory storage

key.desc: Custom cache key for memory MCP data (restore keys are auto-generated by splitting on '-')

description.desc: Optional description for the cache that will be shown in the agent prompt

retention-days.desc: Number of days to retain uploaded artifacts (1-90 days, default: repository setting)

restore-only.desc: If true, only restore the cache without saving it back.

scope.desc: Cache restore key scope: 'workflow' (default, only restores from same workflow) or 'repo' (restores from any workflow...

comment-memory.desc: Comment memory configuration for managed comment persistence

max.desc: Maximum number of comment_memory updates to process (default: 1).

target.desc: Target for comment memory: 'triggering' (default), '*' (current issue/PR), or explicit issue/PR number

target-repo.desc: Target repository in format 'owner/repo' for cross-repository memory storage.

allowed-repos.desc: Additional repositories in format 'owner/repo' allowed for comment memory operations.

memory-id.desc: Default memory identifier when output items omit memory_id.

footer.desc: Controls whether AI-generated footer is added to the managed comment.

github-token.desc: GitHub token to use for comment-memory operations.

staged.desc: If true, emit step summary messages instead of making GitHub API calls for this specific output type (preview mode)

startup-timeout.desc: Timeout in seconds for MCP server startup.

cli-proxy.desc: When true, each user-facing MCP server is mounted as a standalone CLI tool on PATH.

serena.desc: REMOVED: Built-in support for Serena has been removed.

repo-memory.desc: Repo memory configuration for git-based persistent storage

file-glob.desc: Glob patterns for files to include in repository memory.

max-file-size.desc: Maximum size per file in bytes (default: 102400 = 100KB)

max-patch-size.desc: Maximum total patch size in bytes (default: 10240 = 10KB, max: 1048576 = 1MB).

description.desc: Optional description for the memory that will be shown in the agent prompt

wiki.desc: Use the GitHub Wiki git repository instead of the regular repository.

cache.desc: Cache configuration for workflow (uses actions/cache syntax)

key.desc: An explicit key for restoring and saving the cache

path.desc: File path or directory to cache for faster workflow execution.

restore-keys.desc: Optional list of fallback cache key patterns to use if exact cache key is not found.

upload-chunk-size.desc: The chunk size used to split up large files during upload, in bytes

lookup-only.desc: If true, only checks if cache entry exists and skips download

env.desc: Environment variables to pass to safe output jobs
